name: The Automaton Loom
M: w w w w w w w w w w w
w   w w w l2w w w   w
w     w w l1w w     w
w w   w       w w   w
w   w w w w w w w   w
w w w   w   w   w w w
w w w w w   w w w w w

l1.task:
Before it can read three cells at a time, the loom learns to read two. Slide a window of two along `⍵`, catenating each neighbouring pair — an _n-wise reduce_.

`{answer} 0 1 1 0` gives the pairs `(0 1)(1 1)(1 0)`.

l2.task:
The loom hems each thread with a blank at either end. Fringe the row `⍵` with a 0 on each side.

`{answer} 1 0 1` gives `0 1 0 1 0`.

m∪.task: {m∪} is the _emerald rune of distinction_, keeping only the first appearance of each item on its right. The loom has spun the strand `s` thick with repeats; card it down to its distinct threads.

aF.task: _Wolfram rules._ For the world `⍵` — with unseen 0s beyond its ends — a cell lives in the next generation if its three-neighbour pattern is one of the rule-triplets `⍺`. Forge the rune that weaves the next generation of the world.
